item.title: Vulnerability Data Enrichment for CVE Records: 259 CNAs on the Enrichment Recognition List for March 2, 2026
item.description: Vulnerability Data Enrichment for CVE Records: 259 CNAs on the Enrichment Recognition List for March 2, 2026blogBy CVE Program|March 3, 2026

item.title: Cygence Added as CVE Numbering Authority (CNA)
item.description: Cygence Added as CVE Numbering Authority (CNA)newsMarch 3, 2026Cygence Pty Ltdis now aCVE Numbering Authority (CNA)for issues in third-party products identified by or reported to Cygence, unless covered by the scope of another CNA.

item.title: Turan Security Added as CVE Numbering Authority (CNA)
item.description: Turan Security Added as CVE Numbering Authority (CNA)newsMarch 3, 2026Turan Securityis now aCVE Numbering Authority (CNA)for vulnerabilities discovered through independent research conducted by TuranSec to products and projects that are not CNAs themselves.

item.title: Full Agenda Now Available forCVE/FIRST VulnCon 2026on April 13-16, 2026
item.description: Full Agenda Now Available forCVE/FIRST VulnCon 2026on April 13-16, 2026blogBy CVE Program|March 10, 2026CVE/FIRST VulnCon 2026

item.title: Minutes from CVE Board Teleconference Meeting on February 4 Now Available
item.description: Minutes from CVE Board Teleconference Meeting on February 4 Now AvailablenewsMarch 17, 2026TheCVE Boardheld a teleconference meeting on February 4, 2026. Read themeeting minutes summary.

item.title: Bombadil Systems Added as CVE Numbering Authority (CNA)
item.description: Bombadil Systems Added as CVE Numbering Authority (CNA)newsMarch 17, 2026Bombadil Systems LLCis now aCVE Numbering Authority (CNA)for vulnerabilities in third-party software discovered by Bombadil Systems that are not in another CNA’s scope.

item.title: Historical CVE Record Date and Timestamp Normalization Complete!
item.description: Historical CVE Record Date and Timestamp Normalization Complete!blogBy CVE Program|March 24, 2026TheCVE™ Programsuccessfully normalized the formatting of date/time fields across approximately 200,000 historicalCVE Recordson February 16-26, 2026. Asinitially announced on January 6, 2026, and in afollow-on announcement on February 10, 2026, this maintenance updated older CVE Records so their date/time values use the same standardized ISO 8601 UTC format already in place for records published or updated since February 2025:yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.sssZ (ISO-8601, UTC)For example:2025-07-11T19:32:03.983Z

item.title: CVE Podcast — CVE Record Disputes Explained
item.description: CVE Podcast — CVE Record Disputes ExplainedpodcastMarch 24, 2026In this episode of the “We Speak CVE” podcast,CVE ProgramProject Lead Alec Summers chats with Yves Younan ofCisco, Alex Kreilein ofQualys, Pedro Sampaio ofRed Hat, and Anthony Singleton of theMITRE Top-Level Root, about the CVE Record dispute process.

item.title: “Supplier ADP Pilot” — CVE Program to Explore Benefits of Supporting VEX-like, Product Status Information in Upstream CVE Records
item.description: “Supplier ADP Pilot” — CVE Program to Explore Benefits of Supporting VEX-like, Product Status Information in Upstream CVE RecordsblogBy CVE Program|March 24, 2026Software is made of other software. When an upstream component has a vulnerability, downstreamproductsthat include or depend on it may not be similarly affected. Variations in implementation, configuration, environment, and operational context can mean different downstream impacts.

item.title: Cribl Added as CVE Numbering Authority (CNA)
item.description: Cribl Added as CVE Numbering Authority (CNA)newsMarch 24, 2026Cribl, Inc.is now aCVE Numbering Authority (CNA)for vulnerabilities identified in Cribl products, and vulnerabilities discovered by, or reported to, Cribl that are not in another CNA’s scope.
